title: spendable_notes() returns notes from all accounts despite account param
description: db.rs spendable_notes(account) rejects account != 0 in Rust but the SQL query has no account_index filter — it returns ALL unspent notes regardless of account. Trigger: wallet has notes from multiple accounts (currently impossible since multi-account is not supported, but the guard is not in SQL). Bad behavior: when multi-account support lands and a migration adds account_index, this function silently mixes notes from all accounts because the SQL WHERE clause does not filter by account. The Rust guard is the only enforcement and it only blocks account != 0, not cross-account mixing for account 0. The guard comment acknowledges this but the SQL is the wrong place to leave this unfixed — a future migration that adds account_index without updating this query will silently corrupt note selection for account 0 by mixing in other accounts' notes.
close_reason: Already addressed: db.rs lines 558-561 document the limitation and prescribe the fix (add account_index column + WHERE clause) for when multi-account lands. Current single-account guard is correct.
